title: erythrococca subspicata
intro: Erythrococca subspicata, also known as the African poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its shiny black seeds and toxic properties. The plant's seeds contain a toxic compound called erythrine, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Erythrococca subspicata has been found to have medicinal properties that aid in pain relief, fever reduction, and inflammation management. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often dried and crushed to create a powder, which can be used to make teas or infusions that are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and improving wound healing.
